course_title: Quality Coaching: Understanding, Informing and Advancing Practice
course_description: This course aims to broaden your knowledge and understanding of coaching work. You will be provided with opportunities to reflect upon and develop your coaching practices. The course is based around a series of seminars focused on an increased awareness and understanding of (i) what coaching is, (ii) the educational dimensions of coaching work, (iii) coaches as learners in their own right, and (iv) the ways in which coaching work is evaluated.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

course_aims: This course aims to broaden your knowledge and understanding of contemporary forms of sport coaching and provides students with opportunities to challenge and reaffirm aspects of their own / others’ practices.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate a developing awareness and understanding of the concept of quality coaching and the associated pedagogical aspects of coaches work;
Demonstrate a developing awareness and understanding of coaches roles in the learning of athletes;
Demonstrate a developing awareness and understanding of the centrality of learning to coaching practice.

assessment_details.title: Seminar 1 submission
assessment_details.task_description: Students submit a short-form written response that critically and reflectively examines seminar topics, drawing on personal experience and relevant academic literature. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via the relevant Turnitin submission portal on the course Blackboard site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Getting started Review of coaching practice and introductions (week 1) Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 4 (02 Mar - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Coaches' work Seminar and readings (week 2) followed by online discussion (week 3); and the preparation and submission of responses to the seminar questions (due end of week 4). Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 5 To Week 7 (23 Mar - 19 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Coaches as educators Seminar and readings (week 5) followed by online discussion (week 6); and the preparation and submission of coach responses to the seminar questions (due end of week 7). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 8 To Week 10 (20 Apr - 10 May)
learning_activities.topic: Coaches as learners Seminar and readings (week 8) followed by online discussion (week 9); and the preparation and submission of student responses to the seminar questions (due Friday, end of week 10) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 11 To Week 13 (11 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Professionalising and evidencing coaching Seminar and readings (week 11) followed by online discussion (week 12); and the preparation and submission of student responses to the seminar questions (due Friday, end of week 13) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
